skill.name: lead-research-assistant
skill.description: Identifies and qualifies potential sales leads by analyzing a product, target market, company fit signals, and outreach opportunities.
skill.summary: Researches target companies and produces prioritized lead recommendations with outreach strategy.
skill.author: Joseph OBrien

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, filesystem access, weak cryptography, sensitive database, reconnaissance, and obfuscation patterns. Review found Markdown templates, placeholders, and output examples rather than executable code or malicious instructions. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, hidden command execution, or confirmed dangerous behavior was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Detected as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick locations are Markdown code fences and example prompts. They do not execute commands, interpolate user input, or invoke a shell.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are Markdown examples and templates. No executable script, command runner, or user-controlled shell invocation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Business Placeholders Detected as Security Terms
low_findings.description: The Windows SAM and weak cryptography detections are caused by sales and template words such as serviceable market, description, trends, and decision-maker text. No cryptographic operation or sensitive database access appears in context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged strings are embedded in ordinary business research prose and placeholder labels. There is no code path that uses MD5, DES, SAM files, or related security-sensitive resources.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Template Artifact Paths Detected as Filesystem Access
low_findings.description: The /tmp paths appear as suggested research artifact names inside a Markdown report template. The skill does not contain code that reads, writes, or uploads those files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths are displayed as report output examples under a research artifacts heading. No filesystem API, shell command, or automation instruction executes against them.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Website Field Detected as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance finding maps to a lead report field asking for a company website. This is normal public business research, not scanning, probing, or exploit discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line requests a public company website as report metadata. It does not direct network scanning, credential collection, or vulnerability enumeration.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Template Syntax Detected as Obfuscation
low_findings.description: The obfuscation heuristic appears to have matched repeated template placeholder syntax. The referenced file is a Markdown report template, not JavaScript or encoded executable content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains front matter and many double-brace placeholders used for report fields. No JSFuck-like JavaScript or executable payload was found.

content.user_title: Find Qualified Leads with Research Context
content.value_statement: Sales teams need better lead lists than generic company directories. This skill researches company fit, priority signals, and outreach angles for focused prospecting.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes a product or service description to define an ideal customer profile.
Identifies target companies using industry, size, location, technology, growth, and pain point criteria.
Ranks leads with a fit score and explains the priority for each account.
Suggests decision-maker roles, conversation starters, and outreach strategies.
Uses report templates for research summaries and competitive analysis.
limitations: It depends on available public information and user-provided product context.
It does not guarantee contact accuracy or sales conversion outcomes.
It should not be used to collect private personal data or bypass access controls.
It may need human review before CRM import or customer outreach.

use_cases.title: Build a Target Account List
use_cases.description: Create a prioritized list of companies that match a product, market, and customer profile.

use_cases.title: Plan Partnership Outreach
use_cases.description: Find companies with complementary products and define a clear reason to start a business conversation.

use_cases.title: Validate a New Market Segment
use_cases.description: Research whether a specific industry or region contains enough strong-fit prospects to justify a campaign.

prompt_templates.title: Find Basic Leads
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with a short product description.
prompt_templates.prompt: I sell [product or service]. Find 10 companies in [industry or location] that could be strong leads.

prompt_templates.title: Use an Ideal Customer Profile
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused prospecting with clear qualification criteria.
prompt_templates.prompt: My product helps [target customer] solve [problem]. Find 20 leads with company size, industry, location, fit score, and outreach angle.

prompt_templates.title: Research from a Repository
prompt_templates.scenario: Use product context from an accessible codebase or project folder.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this repository to understand the product. Then identify companies that would benefit from it and explain why each one is a fit.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Account Strategy
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced account planning for a selected segment.
prompt_templates.prompt: Research [market segment] for [product]. Rank leads, identify decision-maker roles, summarize buying triggers, and propose first-touch messaging for each account.

output_examples.input: Find leads for a security tool that redacts sensitive data from AI coding assistant prompts.
output: A ranked list of fintech, healthcare, and legal technology companies using AI development tools.
Each lead includes fit rationale, priority score, target role, and a tailored conversation starter.

output_examples.input: Research local companies that recently adopted remote work and may need productivity consulting.
output: A regional prospect list with remote hiring signals, likely pain points, and recommended outreach angles.
The report separates high-priority accounts from lower-fit companies.

output_examples.input: Compare competitors before choosing prospects for a new developer tool.
output: A market overview with competitor positioning, feature gaps, and target segments.
The recommendations explain which account types are most likely to value the product.

best_practices: Provide the product value proposition, target industry, location, and company size constraints.
Ask for evidence behind each lead score so the list can be reviewed before outreach.
Verify contact details and compliance requirements before importing leads into a CRM.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for private personal data, scraped credentials, or non-public contact information.
Do not treat generated lead scores as final without human qualification.
Do not run outreach from stale research without checking recent company context.

faq.question: What does this skill produce?
faq.answer: It produces lead lists with company context, fit scores, target roles, and outreach strategy.

faq.question: Can it analyze my product from a codebase?
faq.answer: Yes. The instructions support reviewing an accessible repository to understand the product before researching leads.

faq.question: Does it find personal email addresses?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on company research, decision-maker roles, and public professional context. Users should follow privacy and outreach laws.

faq.question: Can it create competitive analysis?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a competitive analysis template for market overview, competitor profiles, feature comparison, and recommendations.

faq.question: Is the output ready for a CRM?
faq.answer: It can suggest CSV-ready lead fields, but users should verify accuracy before importing or contacting prospects.

faq.question: Which tools support this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
